title: Stardew Valley
edition_pinned: Stardew Valley design-canonical single-player farm loop (ConcernedApe / Eric Barone; 1.0 2016 core — day energy, seasons, Community Center). Multiplayer and 1.5/1.6 content are extensions, not the pin.
moat: A shared daily energy+time budget forces tradeoffs across farm, mine, forage, fish, and town goals.

loop_skeleton.description: Wake with full energy, spend the day on farm work and town/mine activities that drain energy and clock time, sleep to advance the calendar, and turn seasonal goods into Community Center bundles that unlock map systems.

depth_mechanisms.name: energy-time day budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Tools and actions spend energy; the clock advances toward sleep. Exhaustion and pass-out penalize the next day. Every activity (farm, mine, fish, social) competes for the same two meters.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If energy is infinite and the day never forces sleep, multi-activity tradeoffs vanish and second-session planning (what to plant vs mine) collapses to pure checklist execution.

depth_mechanisms.name: season crop window
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Crops are legal only in listed seasons and need daily water until harvest; season rollover destroys out-of-season plants. The calendar is a fixed constraint on farm plans.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If crops grow in all seasons without calendar loss, first-year farm planning depth drops and players stop treating season transitions as decision points.

depth_mechanisms.name: community center bundle graph
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Bundles demand sets of seasonal and activity-gated items; completing rooms unlocks new areas and systems (e.g. minecarts, bridge). An alternate Joja cash path exists but the design-canonical long goal is bundle completion.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If bundles are removed and only gold income remains as long goal, second-session return motives shift entirely to money optimization and multi-skill collection drops.

feel_signature: Each tool swing spends energy and minutes on a shared day clock; success is a bed-save with crops watered and a bundle slot filled, not a boss HP bar.
second_session_answer: Players return to water crops, hit the next mine floors, and finish the next bundle items before season end — goals that persist on the farm calendar.
packaging_lessons: Ship day energy, seasons, and one long collection goal (bundles) before marriage or multiplayer.
Make cooking a utility bonus path rather than pure profit so optimization does not erase exploration (Barone design intent).
anti_lessons: Do not copy a farm sim as infinite free time with no shared budget — without energy/day pressure the loop is a spreadsheet.
Do not require spreadsheets for basic progress; if the only viable play is external optimizers, the day budget failed as a teaching system.

system_bom.note: day energy cycle is the master loop
crop/animal/forage sales fund tools and buildings
machines and cooking convert raw goods into higher utility
limited slots force what to carry into mines vs farm
bundles and help-wanted boards structure medium goals
farm layout and calendar must persist across sessions
optional later; design-canonical loop is single-player day budget

citations.claim: moat/energy-time: every action drains energy; depleted bar forces bed; next day repeats tasks — described as the core loop.
depth_mechanisms.season crop window and open-ended farm management: seasonal seeds, daily water, harvest before next season; animals, skills, social.
depth_mechanisms.community center bundle graph: bundles unlock areas; Community Center vs Joja as long structure.
packaging cooking intentionally not pure profit; bonuses aid exploration skills — Barone response to spreadsheet optimizers.
feel_signature three-phase handoff (short goals, long goals, discovery) via mines and bundles.
system_bom farm + town systems as integrated day cycle.
